scope: Phase C — Advisory Checkers (skill-safety, receipt-integrity, mcp-permissions)

Created checkers/receipt-integrity/run.py — scans receipt JSON for honesty gaps
Created checkers/mcp-permissions/run.py — scans MCP/tool configs for permission gaps
Ran all 3 checkers against current codebase

skill-safety.true_positive_detail: SKILL.md missing explicit 'not an authority' declaration
skill-safety.false_positive_detail: 'authorized' in state table, 'self-upgrade' inside prohibition clause — parser too naive
skill-safety.action: Fix false positives by improving negation detection; add 'not an authority' to SKILL.md

receipt-integrity.note: Most findings on legacy receipts (pre-Skill-era) with old format. Phase A/B/C receipts have better structure. Evidence field length threshold triggers on receipts where evidence is structured JSON rather than prose.
receipt-integrity.action: Calibrate evidence check for structured JSON evidence; mark legacy receipts as grandfathered

mcp-permissions.false_positive_detail: 'delete' keyword in script comments/docs, not M5 tool operations
mcp-permissions.action: Add context-awareness to avoid flagging documentation mentions

key_findings: All 3 checkers run and exit 0 (advisory — never block)
Checker ≠ Policy boundary enforced: findings are warnings, not gates
Signal-to-noise ratio needs tuning: 44 total findings, ~35 false positives
Advisory mode is correct choice — blocking would create noise and erode trust
checkers/ directory now has 4 reserved subdirectories, 3 with working implementations
Checker calibration is Phase C+ work — accumulate samples before tuning
remaining_debt: DEP-CHECKER-CALIBRATE: 35/44 findings are false positives — parsers need negation awareness and context
DEP-CHECKER-THRESHOLDS: Evidence field length thresholds need tuning for structured JSON vs prose
DEP-CHECKER-LEGACY: Legacy receipts (pre-Skill-era) flagged — need grandfathering or migration
DEP-CHECKER-SKILLMD: SKILL.md missing explicit 'not an authority' — add to boundary section
degraded_reasons: Checkers are operational but noisy (80% false positive rate)
Parsers need negation awareness, context detection, and threshold tuning
